simple_types.name: Lua_Arith_Op
simple_types.code:
type Lua_Arith_Op is
  (LUA_OPADD,
   LUA_OPSUB,
   LUA_OPMUL,
   LUA_OPDIV,
   LUA_OPMOD,
   LUA_OPPOW,
   LUA_OPUNM);

simple_types.name: Lua_Return_Code
simple_types.code:
type Lua_Return_Code is
  (LUA_OK,
   LUA_YIELD,
   LUA_ERRRUN,
   LUA_ERRSYNTAX,
   LUA_ERRMEM,
   LUA_ERRGCMM,
   LUA_ERRERR,
   LUA_ERRFILE);

documentation.description: Status types
Lua state is the structure containing the state of the interpreter.

simple_types.name: Lua_Type
simple_types.code:
type Lua_Type is
  (LUA_TNONE,
   LUA_TNIL,
   LUA_TBOOLEAN,
   LUA_TLIGHTUSERDATA,
   LUA_TNUMBER,
   LUA_TSTRING,
   LUA_TTABLE,
   LUA_TFUNCTION,
   LUA_TUSERDATA,
   LUA_TTHREAD);

documentation.description: Lua types

access_types.name: Lua_Function
access_types.code: type Lua_Function is access function (State : Lua_State) return Integer;

subprograms.name: Absolute_Index
subprograms.code:
function Absolute_Index
  (State : Lua_State;
   Index : Lua_Index)
   return Lua_Index

documentation.description:
Converts the acceptable index idx into an absolute index (that is, one
that does not depend on the stack top).

subprograms.name: Arith
subprograms.code: procedure Arith (State : Lua_State; Operator : Lua_Arith_Op)

documentation.description:
Performs an arithmetic operation over the two values (or one, in the
case of negation) at the top of the stack, with the value at the top
being the second operand, pops these values, and pushes the result of
the operation. The function follows the semantics of the corresponding
Lua operator (that is, it may call metamethods).

subprograms.name: At_Panic
Call
Check_Stack
subprograms.code: function Check_Stack (State : Lua_State; N : Integer) return Boolean

documentation.description:
Ensures that there are at least extra free stack slots in the stack. It
returns False if it cannot fulfill the request, because it would cause
the stack to be larger than a fixed maximum size (typically at least a
few thousand elements) or because it cannot allocate memory for the new
stack size. This function never shrinks the stack; if the stack is
already larger than the new size, it is left unchanged.

subprograms.name: Check_User_Data

documentation.description:
Check if object at position Index has a metatable and it is the same as
metatable stored at Name entry in the registry. In case the object has
not metatable or the metatables do not correspond then return null and
set an error in the current state. Otherwise return the address of the
user data of the object.

subprograms.name: Close
subprograms.code: procedure Close (State : Lua_State)

documentation.description:
Destroys all objects in the given Lua state (calling the corresponding
garbage - collection metamethods, if any) and frees all dynamic memory
used by this state. On several platforms, you may not need to call this
function, because all resources are naturally released when the host
program ends. On the other hand, long - running programs that create
multiple states, such as daemons or web servers, might need to close
states as soon as they are not needed.

subprograms.name: Compare
Concat
subprograms.code: procedure Concat (State : Lua_State; N : Integer)

documentation.description:
Concatenates the n values at the top of the stack, pops them, and leaves
the result at the top. If n is 1, the result is the single value on the
stack (that is, the function does nothing); if n is 0, the result is the
empty string. Concatenation is performed following the usual semantics
of Lua.

subprograms.name: Copy

documentation.description:
Moves the element at index from into the valid index to without
shifting any element (therefore replacing the value at that position).

subprograms.name: Create_Table
Error
subprograms.code: function Error (State : Lua_State) return Integer

documentation.description:
Generates a Lua error. The error message (which can actually be a Lua
value of any type) must be on the stack top. This function does a long
jump, and therefore never returns.

subprograms.name: Get_Field

documentation.description:
Pushes onto the stack the value t[k], where t is the value at the given
index. As in Lua, this function may trigger a metamethod for the "index"
event

subprograms.name: Get_Global

documentation.description: Pushes onto the stack the value of the global name.

subprograms.name: Get_Metatable
Get_Metatable

documentation.description:
Push on top of the stack the metatable registered at entry Name in the
registry

subprograms.name: Get_Table
subprograms.code: procedure Get_Table (State : Lua_State; Index : Lua_Index)

documentation.description:
Pushes onto the stack the value t[k], where t is the value at the given
index and k is the value at the top of the stack.

This function pops the key from the stack (putting the resulting value
in its place). As in Lua, this function may trigger a metamethod for the
"index" event

subprograms.name: Get_Top
subprograms.code: function Get_Top (State : Lua_State) return Lua_Index

documentation.description:
Returns the index of the top element in the stack. Because indices start
at 1, this result is equal to the number of elements in the stack (and
so 0 means an empty stack).

subprograms.name: Get_Type
subprograms.code:
function Get_Type
  (State : Lua_State;
   Index : Lua_Index)
   return Lua_Type

documentation.description:
Returns the type of the value in the given valid index, or LUA_TNONE
for a non - valid (but acceptable) index.

subprograms.name: Get_User_Value
subprograms.code: procedure Get_User_Value (State : Lua_State; Index : Lua_Index)

documentation.description:
Pushes onto the stack the Lua value associated with the userdata at
the given index. This Lua value must be a table or nil.

subprograms.name: Insert
subprograms.code: procedure Insert (State : Lua_State; Index : Lua_Index)

documentation.description:
Moves the top element into the given valid index, shifting up the
elements above this index to open space. This function cannot be called
with a pseudo - index, because a pseudo - index is not an actual stack
position.

subprograms.name: Is_Function
subprograms.code: function Is_Function (State : Lua_State; Index : Lua_Index) return Boolean

documentation.description:
Returns 1 if the value at the given index is a C function, and 0
otherwise.

subprograms.name: Is_Number
subprograms.code: function Is_Number (State : Lua_State; Index : Lua_Index) return Boolean

documentation.description:
Returns 1 if the value at the given index is a number or a string
convertible to a number, and 0 otherwise.

subprograms.name: Is_String
subprograms.code: function Is_String (State : Lua_State; Index : Lua_Index) return Boolean

documentation.description:
Returns 1 if the value at the given index is a string or a number
(which is always convertible to a string), and 0 otherwise.

subprograms.name: Is_User_Data
subprograms.code: function Is_User_Data (State : Lua_State; Index : Lua_Index) return Boolean

documentation.description:
Returns 1 if the value at the given index is a userdata (either full or
light), and 0 otherwise.

subprograms.name: Len
subprograms.code: procedure Len (State : Lua_State; Index : Lua_Index)

subprograms.name: Load_File

documentation.description:
Loads a file as a Lua chunk. This function uses Load to load the
chunk in the file named filename. If filename is NULL, then it loads
from the standard input. The first line in the file is ignored if
it starts with a #.

The string mode works as in function Load.

This function returns the same results as Load, but it has an extra
error code LUA_ERRFILE if it cannot open / read the file or the file
has a wrong mode.

As lua_load, this function only loads the chunk; it does not run it.

subprograms.name: Load_File

documentation.description:
Same as previous function except that Lua_Error exception is raised in
LUA_OK is not returned.

subprograms.name: Load_String

documentation.description: Load a string and raise Lua_Error in case of error

subprograms.name: New_Metatable
subprograms.code: function New_Metatable (State : Lua_State; Name : String) return Boolean

documentation.description:
Create a new metable in the registry called Name. If the metatable is
already there return False, else True. Note that in all cases the
metatable is pushed at the top of the stack

subprograms.name: New_Metatable
subprograms.code: procedure New_Metatable (State : Lua_State; Name : String)

documentation.description: Same as above but no status is returned

subprograms.name: New_State
subprograms.code: function New_State return Lua_State

documentation.description:
Create a new state using the default memory allocator. See Lua.Advanced
in case you want to use custom allocators.

subprograms.name: New_Thread
subprograms.code: function New_Thread (State : Lua_State) return Lua_State

documentation.description:
Creates a new thread, pushes it on the stack, and returns a pointer to
a lua_State that represents this new thread. The new thread returned by
this function shares with the original thread its global environment,
but has an independent execution stack.

There is no explicit function to close or to destroy a thread. Threads
are subject to garbage collection, like any Lua object.

subprograms.name: New_User_Data
subprograms.code:
function New_User_Data
  (State : Lua_State;
   Size  : size_t)
   return System.Address

subprograms.name: Next
subprograms.code: function Next (State : Lua_State; Index : Lua_Index) return Boolean

documentation.description:
Pops a key from the stack, and pushes a key value pair from the table
at the given index (the "next" pair after the given key). If there are
no more elements in the table, then lua_next returns False (and pushes
nothing).

subprograms.name: Open_Libs
subprograms.code: procedure Open_Libs (State : Lua_State)

documentation.description: Opens all standard Lua libraries into the given state.

subprograms.name: PCall
PCall

documentation.description:
Same as PCall function except that a Lua_Error exception is raised in
case of error.

subprograms.name: Pop

documentation.description: Pops n elements from the stack.

subprograms.name: Push
subprograms.code: procedure Push (State : Lua_State)

documentation.description: Pushes a nil value onto the stack.

subprograms.name: Push
subprograms.code: procedure Push (State : Lua_State; Data : Lua_Float)

documentation.description: Pushes a lua float onto the stack.

subprograms.name: Push
subprograms.code: procedure Push (State : Lua_State; Data : Lua_Integer)

documentation.description: Pushes a number with value n onto the stack.

subprograms.name: Push
subprograms.code: procedure Push (State : Lua_State; Data : Lua_Light_User_Data)

documentation.description:
Pushes a light userdata onto the stack. Userdata represent C values in
Lua. A light userdata represents a pointer, a void * . It is a value
(like a number) : you do not create it, it has no individual metatable,
and it is not collected (as it was never created). A light userdata is
equal to "any" light userdata with the same C address.

subprograms.name: Push
subprograms.code: procedure Push (State : Lua_State; Data : Lua_Unsigned)

documentation.description: Pushes a number with value n onto the stack.

subprograms.name: Push
subprograms.code: procedure Push (State : Lua_State; Data : Boolean)

documentation.description: Pushes a boolean value with value b onto the stack.

subprograms.name: Push
subprograms.code: procedure Push (State : Lua_State; Data : String)

documentation.description: Push a string on the stack

subprograms.name: Push_Closure
Push_Thread
subprograms.code: function Push_Thread (State : Lua_State) return Integer

documentation.description:
Pushes the thread represented by L onto the stack. Returns 1 if this
thread is the main thread of its state.

subprograms.name: Push_Value
subprograms.code: procedure Push_Value (State : Lua_State; Index : Lua_Index)

documentation.description: Pushes a copy of the element at the given index onto the stack.

subprograms.name: Raw_Equal

documentation.description:
Returns 1 if the two values in indices index1 and index2 are
primitively equal (that is, without calling metamethods). Otherwise
returns 0. Also returns 0 if any of the indices are non valid.

subprograms.name: Raw_Get
subprograms.code: procedure Raw_Get (State : Lua_State; Index : Lua_Index)

documentation.description:
Similar to lua_gettable, but does a raw access (i.e., without
metamethods).

subprograms.name: Raw_Geti

documentation.description:
Pushes onto the stack the value t[n], where t is the table at the given
index. The access is raw; that is, it does not invoke metamethods.

subprograms.name: Raw_Len
subprograms.code: function Raw_Len (State : Lua_State; Index : Lua_Index) return size_t

documentation.description:
Returns the raw "length" of the value at the given index: for strings,
this is the string length; for tables, this is the result of the length
operator ('#') with no metamethods; for userdata, this is the size of
the block of memory allocated for the userdata; for other values,
it is 0.

subprograms.name: Raw_Set
subprograms.code: procedure Raw_Set (State : Lua_State; Index : Lua_Index)

documentation.description:
Similar to lua_settable, but does a raw assignment (i.e.,
without metamethods).

subprograms.name: Raw_Seti

documentation.description:
Does the equivalent of t[n] = v, where t is the table at the given
index and v is the value at the top of the stack.

This function pops the value from the stack. The assignment is raw;
that is, it does not invoke metamethods.

subprograms.name: Register_Function

documentation.description:
Same as previous function except that the registered object is a
closure passed as argument to the function instead of using the stack

subprograms.name: Register_Object

documentation.description:
Helper function to register an Ada object inside lua state. Name is the
name in Lua global environment with which the function is associated to.
To ease creation of hierarchies in the global environment, if Name
contains '.' then a hierarchy using Lua tables is created. For example:

   Register (S, "a.b.c");

will create a global table called "a". The table "a" will contain a
table at index "b" and this last table will contain one element "b" set
to the object on top of the stack. Note that an error will be raised
in case you try to register twice at the same location.

subprograms.name: Remove
subprograms.code: procedure Remove (State : Lua_State; Index : Lua_Index)

documentation.description:
Removes the element at the given valid index, shifting down the elements
above this index to fill the gap. This function cannot be called with a
pseudo - index, because a pseudo - index is not an actual stack
position.

subprograms.name: Replace
subprograms.code: procedure Replace (State : Lua_State; Index : Lua_Index)

documentation.description:
Moves the top element into the given valid index without shifting any
element (therefore replacing the value at the given index), and then
pops the top element.

subprograms.name: Resume

documentation.description: Starts and resumes a coroutine in a given thread.

subprograms.name: Rotate

documentation.description:
Moves the value at index From to index To
These values will be swapped

subprograms.name: Set_Field
Set_Field

documentation.description:
Does the equivalent to t[k] = v, where t is the value at the given
index and v is the value at the top of the stack.

This function pops the value from the stack. As in Lua, this function
may trigger a metamethod for the "newindex" event

subprograms.name: Set_Global

documentation.description: Pops a value from the stack and sets it as the new value of global name.

subprograms.name: Set_Metatable
subprograms.code: procedure Set_Metatable (State : Lua_State; Index : Lua_Index)

documentation.description:
Pop a table from the stack and sets it as the new metatable for
the value at the given index.

subprograms.name: Set_Metatable

documentation.description:
Sets the metatable of the object at the top of the stack as the
metatable associated with name Name in the registry

subprograms.name: Set_Table

documentation.description:
Does the equivalent to t[k] = v, where t is the value at the given
index, v is the value at the top of the stack, and k is the value just
below the top.

This function pops both the key and the value from the stack. As in Lua,
this function may trigger a metamethod for the "newindex" event

subprograms.name: Set_Top

documentation.description:
Accepts any index, or 0, and sets the stack top to this index. If the
new top is larger than the old one, then the new elements are filled
with nil. If index is 0, then all stack elements are removed.

subprograms.name: Set_User_Value
subprograms.code:
procedure Set_User_Value (State : Lua_State;
                          Index : Lua_Index)

documentation.description:
Pops a table or nil from the stack and sets it as the new value
associated to the userdata at the given index.

subprograms.name: Status
subprograms.code: function Status (State : Lua_State) return Lua_Return_Code

subprograms.name: Test_User_Data

documentation.description:
Same as previous function except that no error object is pushed on the
stack.

subprograms.name: To_Ada
subprograms.code:
function To_Ada
  (State   : Lua_State;
   Index   : Lua_Index)
   return Lua_Float

documentation.description:
Converts the Lua value at the given index to the Ada type Lua_Float
The Lua value must be a number or a string convertible to a number
otherwise, raise Lua_Type_Error.

subprograms.name: To_Ada
subprograms.code:
function To_Ada
  (State : Lua_State;
   Index : Lua_Index)
   return Lua_Function

documentation.description:
Converts a value at the given index to a C function. That value must be
a C function; otherwise, returns NULL.

subprograms.name: To_Ada
subprograms.code:
function To_Ada
  (State   : Lua_State;
   Index   : Lua_Index)
   return Lua_Integer

documentation.description:
Converts the Lua value at the given index to the Ada type Lua_Integer
The Lua value must be a number or a string convertible to a number
otherwise, raise Lua_Type_Error.

subprograms.name: To_Ada
subprograms.code:
function To_Ada
  (State : Lua_State;
   Index : Lua_Index)
   return Lua_State

documentation.description:
Converts the value at the given index to a Lua thread (represented as
Lua_State). This value must be a thread; otherwise, the function
returns NULL.

subprograms.name: To_Ada
subprograms.code:
function To_Ada
  (State  : Lua_State;
   Index  : Lua_Index)
   return Lua_Unsigned

documentation.description:
Converts the Lua value at the given index to the Ada type Lua_Unsigned
The Lua value must be a number or a string convertible to a number
otherwise, raise Lua_Type_Error.

subprograms.name: To_Ada
subprograms.code:
function To_Ada
  (State : Lua_State;
   Index : Lua_Index)
   return Lua_User_Data

documentation.description:
If the value at the given index is a full userdata, returns its block
address. If the value is a light userdata, returns its pointer.
Otherwise, returns NULL.

subprograms.name: To_Ada
subprograms.code:
function To_Ada
  (State : Lua_State;
   Index : Lua_Index)
   return Boolean

documentation.description:
Converts the Lua value at the given index to a boolean value.
Like all tests in Lua, To_Boolean returns true for any Lua value
different from false and nil; otherwise it returns false. (If you want
to accept only actual boolean values, use Is_Boolean to test the
value's type.)

subprograms.name: To_Ada
subprograms.code:
function To_Ada
  (State : Lua_State;
   Index : Lua_Index)
   return String

documentation.description:
Converts the Lua value at the given index to a Ada string. The Lua value
must be a string or a number; otherwise, the function raise
Lua_Type_Error. If the value is a number, then lua_tolstring also
changes the actual value in the stack to a string. (This change
confuses lua_next when lua_tolstring is applied to keys during a table
traversal.)

subprograms.name: Type_Name
subprograms.code:
function Type_Name
  (State : Lua_State;
   Index : Lua_Index)
   return String

documentation.description:
Returns the name of the type encoded by the value tp, which must be
one the values returned by lua_type.

subprograms.name: Upvalue_Index
subprograms.code: function Upvalue_Index (N : Positive) return Lua_Index

subprograms.name: Version
subprograms.code: function Version (State : Lua_State) return access Lua_Float

subprograms.name: X_Move

documentation.description:
Exchange values between different threads of the same state.

This function pops n values from the stack from, and pushes them onto
the stack to.

subprograms.name: Yield
